commit.message:
fix(state): deadlock in load_adapter_name_cache — non-reentrant lock acquired twice

get_adapter_name() acquires _adapter_cache_lock then calls
load_adapter_name_cache() which tried to acquire the same lock.
threading.Lock is not reentrant → deadlock on first /api/status call.

Remove the lock from load_adapter_name_cache() body; callers are
responsible for holding _adapter_cache_lock.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent crash on ':' with noice.nvim/LazyVim

Three targeted fixes for the hard crash when pressing ':' in a LazyVim
environment that uses noice.nvim:

1. auto_load defaults to false
   Async git/gh jobs no longer start automatically when opening a file.
   This eliminates the race where vim.schedule callbacks (setting extmarks)
   fire mid-CmdlineEnter while noice.nvim is rendering its cmdline float.
   Users who want auto-load must opt in: setup({ auto_load = true }).

2. signs.place() skips during cmdline/operator-pending mode
   Added a vim.api.nvim_get_mode() guard — if the mode starts with 'c'
   (cmdline) or 'r' (replace/operator), extmark API calls are skipped.
   This is a safety net for any callbacks that still fire during those modes.

3. BufReadPost auto-load uses vim.defer_fn(fn, 500) instead of vim.schedule
   Deferring 500 ms after BufReadPost ensures the buffer is fully settled
   and the initial UI render (including noice.nvim's startup processing)
   is complete before any async jobs are started.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(kernels): eliminate causal mask allocation in AttentionWorkspace (#3331)

## What changed
- Add \causal_mask_fill()\ that writes into a pre-allocated buffer
- Refactor \causal_mask()\ to delegate to \causal_mask_fill()\
- Add \mask\ buffer to \AttentionWorkspace\
- \multi_head_attention_with_workspace()\ now reuses the mask buffer
instead of allocating a new \Vec<f32>\ on every forward pass

## Impact
Saves O(seq_len) allocation per forward pass when using the workspace
path. For seq_len=2048, that's 16MB of allocation avoided per forward
pass.

## Receipt
- \cargo check -p bitnet-kernels --no-default-features --features cpu\
(passed locally)
- Full test validation delegated to CI Build & Test
- No parity concerns: same mask values, just written into a reusable
buffer
- Docs touched: none
- Risks: none strictly additive; existing \causal_mask()\ API preserved
unchanged
- Recommended disposition: **A) MERGE NOW**

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  auto-executed Session Start prompt (TUI stays visible in tmux panes)

  escalation for copilot (falls through to explicit nudge)

  gunshi→command.0, ashigaru1-7→agents.0-6 (was agents.0-8 mixed)

  cli_adapter.sh instead of hardcoded copilot --yolo

commit.message:
fix(pulse): revert thread-local event loop — caused Waitress thread starvation

pulsectl_asyncio leaves resources on the loop; reusing it blocks
subsequent run_until_complete() calls, starving all Waitress workers.
Revert to fresh event loop per sync call.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Migrate to OpenMRS Core 3.0.0-SNAPSHOT (#708)

Key changes:
- Bump module version to 4.0.0-SNAPSHOT
- Upgrade Java from 8 to 21
- javax.servlet -> jakarta.servlet namespace migration
- org.apache.commons.lang -> org.apache.commons.lang3
- Jackson 1.x (org.codehaus.jackson) -> Jackson 2.x (com.fasterxml.jackson)
- Hibernate Criteria API -> JPA Criteria API in RestHelperServiceImpl
- Spring 6: CommonsMultipartResolver -> StandardServletMultipartResolver
- Spring 6: PathPatternParser compatibility
- Hibernate 6: Cache API updates, Query API changes, strict lazy loading
- Fix Core 3.0 API removals (Field.getAnswers, setStatus(int,String), etc.)
- Update version patterns in resources/search handlers for Core 3.0
- Fix test datasets for Core 3.0 schema changes (NOT NULL constraints)
- Add -parameters compiler flag for Spring 6 parameter name resolution
- Update CI matrix: Java 21 and 25

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
v0.1.37: remove public libp2p bootstrap nodes

Protocol Labs bootstrap.libp2p.io nodes are useless for our private network:
- They don't speak metaverse-server/relay protocol
- Their IPv6 addrs fail on most clients (os error 101)
- Their WSS addrs fail with UnknownIssuer (no CA roots in client)
- They don't have our server in their DHT namespace

Our own server at 144.6.111.191 (bootstrap.json) is sufficient.
The noise was hidden before v0.1.36; now visible due to proper error logging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove Arrow and CodecZstd Julia dependencies (#2965)

Arrow and CodecZstd were stale dependencies in the Julia core — no
longer used in the codebase but retained due to a prior resolver error.
This removes them properly and re-resolves the manifest.

## Changes

- **`core/Project.toml`** — Remove `Arrow` and `CodecZstd` from `[deps]`
and `[compat]`
- **`core/test/Project.toml`** — Remove `Arrow` and `CodecZstd` from
`[deps]`
- **`Project.toml`** (root workspace) — Remove `Arrow` and `CodecZstd`
from `[deps]`
- **`Manifest.toml`** — Remove `Arrow`, `ArrowTypes`, `BitIntegers`,
`CodecLz4`, `CodecZstd`, `StringViews`; strip `ArrowTypes` weak-dep
references from `InlineStrings` and `JSON3`
- **`core/test/aqua_test.jl`** — Drop the stale-dep ignore list now that
the packages are gone:
  ```julia
  # before
  stale_deps = (ignore = [:Arrow, :CodecZstd],),
  # after
  stale_deps = true,
  ```
- **`core/src/read.jl`** — Update outdated docstring that referenced
Arrow as a supported input format

commit.message:
feat: orchestration, token optimizer, e-commerce phase, DB storage, openai v1 automation

Phase 2 — Prompt chain orchestration:
- Add core/orchestration.py: PromptChainOrchestrator runs stages in order,
  passes shared context between stages, retries with exponential back-off
  (configurable max_retries + retry_delay_s), fails open so one bad stage
  doesn't abort the whole chain; exposes run_chain() convenience function
- Wire AnalysisPipeline.run() to use run_chain() instead of a manual for-loop
- Add core/token_optimizer.py: estimate_tokens(), get_context_budget(),
  truncate_dataframe() (binary search to max rows), build_prompt() (safe
  format_map that never raises KeyError), check_prompt_size() with optional
  raise_on_exceed; MODEL_CONTEXT_LIMITS dict for gpt-4o/gpt-4/gpt-3.5
- Token optimizer integrated into orchestrator — data truncated once up front
- Load ALL YAML files in prompts/ (not just youtube_goals.yaml) so new
  domain prompt files are picked up automatically

Phase 3 — Database storage:
- Implement _store_results() in weekly_analysis.py using SQLAlchemy;
  creates analysis_reports table + index on first run; upserts on conflict;
  serialises full report as JSON text; graceful error handling

Phase 4 — E-commerce domain:
- Add prompts/ecommerce_goals.yaml: increase_revenue, reduce_cart_abandonment,
  improve_retention — each with goal-specific prompt chains
- Register 3 e-commerce goals in AnalysisPipeline.goal_chains
- Add _identify_metrics() entries for all 3 e-commerce goals
- Add _ingest_shopify() connector stub to IngestPipeline with inline
  example of the Shopify Admin API call pattern

Other fixes:
- Fix automation/github_docs_sync.py: update to openai v1 client API
  (lazy import inside generate_summary, remove stale openai.api_key assignment)
- Export new modules from core/__init__.py: run_chain, PromptChainOrchestrator,
  token_optimizer functions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
build: add .dockerignore for faster Docker builds

Excludes node_modules, .git, .angular, dist, and other unnecessary
files from Docker build context to reduce context size and speed up builds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: comprehensive pipeline improvements

- GHCR registry instead of SCP image transfer (~10x faster deploys)
- Parallel API + frontend image builds via separate jobs
- Deduplicate test runs: deploy triggers on CI completion, not push
- Zero-downtime: rolling recreate instead of compose down+up
- Post-deploy health gate + smoke tests (fail pipeline if unhealthy)
- Manual deploy trigger via workflow_dispatch with SHA override
- GitHub Environments integration for deploy tracking
- Discord webhook notifications on deploy (opt-in via secret)
- Trivy vulnerability scanning on CI Docker builds
- Rollback script for instant SHA-based rollback via GHCR tags
- Flyway versioned DB migrations (replaces manual schema.sql exec)
- Buildx with GHA cache for both API and frontend images

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove registry-url to enable true OIDC Trusted Publishing

setup-node injects NODE_AUTH_TOKEN=XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX-XXXXX when
registry-url is specified. This dummy token prevents npm from falling
back to OIDC for authentication, causing silent publish failures.

Removing registry-url lets npm use its default registry (npmjs.org)
and properly negotiate OIDC auth with the Trusted Publisher config.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix missing pants rendering in 1_6_Designer.json (#47)

* Initial plan

* Fix pants rendering: process groups without paint in forward pass and transform bubbled-up paths

In render_shape_items, groups without paint ops were skipped in Phase 1
(forward pass) and only rendered in Phase 3 (reverse). This meant their
paths were never accumulated before Fill ops took a snapshot, causing
fills that depend on sub-group paths to render nothing.

Fix: Process groups without paint in Phase 1 so their paths are available
for subsequent fills. Also transform bubbled-up paths by the group's local
matrix so they are positioned correctly in the parent coordinate space.

This fixes the missing pants in 1_6_Designer.json where leg layers use
sub-groups (Wedge Group 2, C2 Group) to generate paths that a sibling
Fill should render.

Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: cg-zhou <9943470+cg-zhou@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: CSRF, missing import, type bugs, LLM test mocks, structured logging

Security:
- Fix OAuth CSRF: validate state param in youtube_oauth_callback (main.py)
  before exchanging auth code; clear state cookie after use
- Fix missing 'import requests' in youtube_app/auth.py (revoke_credentials
  would crash with NameError on every call)

Bug fixes:
- Fix weekly_analysis.py: _compile_report, _extract_priority_actions, and
  _generate_metrics_tracking now accept AnalysisResult + List[Action] (not
  Dict) and serialize properly; priority_actions now use real recommendations
  instead of hardcoded stubs
- Fix _generate_metrics_tracking type hint: Dict → Any

Testing:
- Add autouse mock_llm fixture to test_analysis.py so tests run offline
  without an OPENAI_API_KEY (patches core.analysis._call_llm → None)
- Add tests/conftest.py with shared sample_youtube_df fixture and reusable
  mock_llm fixture available to all test modules

Features:
- Add configure_logging() to core/__init__.py with LOG_LEVEL env-var support
  (Phase 2 roadmap: structured logging)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: servicenow-devtools-mcp
repository.description: A developer & debug-focused MCP server for ServiceNow — 36 tools for platform introspection, change intelligence, debugging, investigations, and documentation generation.

commit.message:
feat: system-health check + live verification script + inventory fixes

New check (configs/checks/system-health/check.yaml):
  - 13 items: CPU load (1/5/15m), CPU count, memory (total/available/used),
    root disk (free/total/%free), uptime, hostname, zabbix agent watchdog
  - 8 triggers: CPU load high/warning, memory low/critical, disk 10%/5%,
    agent not running, host rebooted
  - LLD: vfs.fs.discovery with pfree item prototypes + disk space trigger
    prototypes (10% and 5% thresholds)
  - Uses only built-in Zabbix agent keys — no scripts needed
  - All 13 items verified delivering live data (100% hit rate)

New script (scripts/verify_check_values.py):
  - Connects to Zabbix API, checks if template is linked to the host,
    queries item.get + history.get for all expected keys
  - Pretty-prints live values with human-readable units and data age
  - Shows LLD-discovered items with data
  - --wait <secs> flag to wait for first data points

Bug fixes:
  zbx/deployer.py:
    - char/log/text value_type items now send trends='0' (Zabbix rejects any
      other value for non-numeric item types) — fixes apply failure
  zbx/commands/inventory.py:
    - _compute_inventory_diff now compares templates too (not just groups/status)
    - _update_host applies template changes via host.update
    - Diff message now shows what specifically changed (groups/templates/status)

inventory.yaml:
  - Added templates: [system-health] to zabbixtest3100

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix SID speed detection: analyze note changes, not all register changes

Previous code checked all 21 SID registers for changes, causing filter
sweeps and volume envelopes to trigger on nearly every frame (speed=1).
Now only tracks note-on/off/pitch events for speed detection, with
histogram-based GCD to handle outlier intervals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: improve performance and robustness across core modules

- pulse.py: reuse thread-local event loop in _run() instead of create/destroy per call
- bluetooth_manager.py: dedicated ThreadPoolExecutor(2) for long-running BT ops (pair, connect, configure)
- bluetooth_manager.py: extract inner D-Bus monitor loop into _inner_dbus_monitor(), replace restart_outer flag with return
- sendspin_client.py: shallow dict copy in DeviceStatus.copy() (all fields immutable, asdict deep-copy unnecessary)
- state.py + routes/api.py: add is_scan_running() guard, reject concurrent BT scans with HTTP 409

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
